title: Family violence leave | Employment New Zealand
content:
## Family violence leave
Eligible employees are entitled to up to 10 days of paid leave each year if they, or a child who lives with them, experience family violence.
- Managing family violence leave Eligible employees can take up to 10 days of paid family violence leave each year if they, or a child living with them has experienced family violence. They can also request short-term flexible working arrangements at any time.

## Managing family violence leave
Eligible employees can take up to 10 days of paid family violence leave each year if they, or a child living with them has experienced family violence. They can also request short-term flexible working arrangements at any time.
- Taking family violence leave If you're affected by family violence, you may be able to take paid leave to get support, move house, go to court or care for children.

## Taking family violence leave
If you're affected by family violence, you may be able to take paid leave to get support, move house, go to court or care for children.
